content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 21, 2004


John C. Wallace
Post Office Box 578
Winters, CA 95694

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-275

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This correspondence is written notification that the Fair Political Practices Commission ("FPPC") has withdrawn your November 18, 2003, request on behalf of John Donlevy, Jr., City Manager for the City of Winters for written advice under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

I left voicemail messages on December 5, 2003, and December 16, 2003, and sent you a letter on December 23, 2003, requesting the additional information necessary to answer your request for advice. I requested a response by January 7, 2004. On January 7, 2004, you sent a fax advising that you were trying to gather the information and asked for an extension until January 8 or 9. To date, I still have not received a response from you. Since we do not have the information necessary to respond to your request, it has been withdrawn. (Regulation 18329 (c)(4)(F).) Once you have gathered the facts necessary, you may submit another request for advice. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\03-275

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 23, 2003

John C. Wallace
Post Office Box 578
Winters, CA 95694

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. ` 03-275

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of John Donlevy, Jr., City
Manager for the City of Winters for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that the Commission
does not provide advice relating to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(3)(A).)

In order to answer your request for advice, more information is necessary. I have
left voicemail messages on December 5, 2003 and December 16, 2003, requesting the
additional information necessary to answer your request for advice and have not received
a response from you. Please provide the following information:

1) Has City Manager Donlevy already participated in decisions regarding the
proposed subdivisions?

2) If not, what is the number of either all property owners or all homeowners in
the jurisdiction or the district the city manager represents?²

3) Also, what is the number of either property owners or homeowners affected
by the decision regarding the proposed subdivision?

4) Additionally, if you wish to rely on the appraisal you included in your request
for advice to show either there will be no reasonably foreseeable effect on
City Manager Donlevy's property or that all of the significant segment will be
affected similarly, it must at least consider the factors listed in regulation
18705.2(b)(1) (copy enclosed).

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² See the enclosed copy of regulation 18707.1(b)(1)(B) for these numbers in context.

File No. 03-275
Page No. 2


As soon as you can send or fax us the additional information, we can proceed to
analyze your questions. Please respond within 15 days or your advice request will be
considered withdrawn. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [illegible]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\03-275

sections.conclusion: Since we do not have the information necessary to respond to your request, it has been withdrawn. (Regulation 18329 (c)(a)@).) Once you have gathered the facts necessary, you may submit another request for advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether City Manager John Donlevy, Jr. of the City of Winters has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding proposed subdivisions affecting his property.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because the requestor failed to provide necessary additional information after multiple requests and missed the January 7, 2004 deadline, though the requestor may resubmit once facts are gathered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the December 23, 2003 initial request for additional information and the January 21, 2004 withdrawal notice. The question concerns conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act related to proposed subdivisions. The withdrawal was based on failure to provide necessary facts within the specified timeframe per Regulation 18329(c)(a)(B).

QUESTION: Whether City Manager John Donlevy, Jr. of the City of Winters has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding proposed subdivisions affecting his property.

CONCLUSION: Since we do not have the information necessary to respond to your request, it has been withdrawn. (Regulation 18329 (c)(a)@).) Once you have gathered the facts necessary, you may submit another request for advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request from John C. Wallace on behalf of City Manager John Donlevy, Jr. regarding conflict-of-interest issues because the requestor failed to provide required additional information despite multiple requests and a missed deadline.
